topics.label: FS.1 Math — linear algebra

subtopics.label: Matrix calculus: derivatives of vector/matrix functions, Jacobians, Hessians
Singular Value Decomposition (SVD): full vs economy, truncated SVD, low-rank approximation
Eigendecomposition: eigenvalues, eigenvectors, diagonalization, matrix powers
QR decomposition: Gram-Schmidt, Householder reflections
Cholesky decomposition: positive definite matrices, LLᵀ
Matrix norms: Frobenius, spectral, nuclear; condition number

topics.label: FS.1 Math — probability & statistics

subtopics.label: Bayesian inference: prior, likelihood, posterior, conjugate priors, MAP estimation
Monte Carlo methods: rejection sampling, importance sampling, MCMC (Metropolis-Hastings, HMC)
Information theory: entropy, cross-entropy, KL-divergence, mutual information, Jensen-Shannon divergence
Hypothesis testing: null/alternative, p-value, type I/II error, power analysis, multiple testing correction
Distributions: Gaussian, Bernoulli, Binomial, Poisson, Beta, Gamma, Dirichlet — properties and conjugacy
Exponential family: sufficient statistics, natural parameters, link functions

topics.label: FS.1 Math — optimization

subtopics.label: Convex optimization: convex sets/functions, gradient descent, Newton's method, BFGS, L-BFGS
Constrained optimization: Lagrange multipliers, KKT conditions, duality, penalty methods
Stochastic methods: SGD, momentum (Nesterov), Adam, AdamW, RMSprop, learning rate schedules
Line search: Armijo condition, Wolfe conditions, backtracking
Proximal methods: proximal gradient, ADMM, proximal operators
Global optimization: simulated annealing, evolutionary strategies, Bayesian optimization

topics.label: FS.1 Math — numerical methods

subtopics.label: Floating-point arithmetic: IEEE 754, precision, overflow/underflow, catastrophic cancellation
Numerical integration: Newton-Cotes, Gaussian quadrature, Monte Carlo integration
Root-finding: Newton-Raphson, secant method, bisection, Brent's method
ODE solvers: Euler, RK4, adaptive step (Dormand-Prince), symplectic integrators (Verlet)
Interpolation: polynomial, spline (cubic), barycentric
Fast Fourier Transform: Cooley-Tukey algorithm, applications in convolution/spectral methods

topics.label: FS.2 Computer systems — OS

subtopics.label: Processes: PCB, context switch, fork/exec/wait, process states, zombie/orphan processes
Threads: kernel vs user threads, pthreads, thread-local storage, thread pools
Virtual memory: page tables (multi-level), TLB, page faults, swapping, mmap, huge pages
File systems: inodes, ext4 layout, journaling, FUSE, VFS layer, directory structure
Scheduling: CFS, nice values, cgroups, real-time classes (SCHED_FIFO, SCHED_RR, SCHED_DEADLINE)
IPC: pipes, fifo, sockets, shared memory (shmget/mmap), semaphores, signals

topics.label: FS.2 C++ systems programming

subtopics.label: LearnCPP chapters 1-8: fundamentals, functions, arrays, pointers, control flow, debugging
LearnCPP chapters 9-12: compound types, enums, structs, classes, inheritance, virtual functions
LearnCPP chapters 13-17: templates, exceptions, smart pointers, move semantics, STL
C++ memory model: allocators, placement new, custom allocators, memory pools
Compile-time metaprogramming: consteval, constexpr, SFINAE, concepts, type traits

topics.label: FS.2 Computer systems — architecture

subtopics.label: CPU pipeline: fetch, decode, execute, memory, writeback; hazards (data, control, structural)
Branch prediction: 2-bit saturating counter, correlating predictors, BTB, RAS
Out-of-order execution: Tomasulo algorithm, reorder buffer, reservation stations
Cache hierarchy: L1/L2/L3, inclusivity, associativity, replacement policy (LRU, pseudo-LRU)
Cache coherence: MESI protocol, MOESI, directory-based, snooping
NUMA: topology, memory latency, first-touch policy, thread/process binding (numactl)
IEEE 754: floating-point representation (single/double), rounding modes, subnormals, special values, precision vs accuracy

topics.label: FS.2 Computer systems — GPU architecture

subtopics.label: Streaming Multiprocessor (SM): warp schedulers, warp divergence, convergence patterns
Memory hierarchy: registers, shared memory (banks, bank conflicts), L1/L2 cache, global memory (HBM/HBM2/HBM3)
Occupancy: register pressure, shared memory per SM, scheduling, occupancy calculator
Tensor cores: 4x4x4 MMA, precision modes (fp16, bf16, tf32, int8, int4), throughput
SM generations: Maxwell → Pascal → Volta → Turing → Ampere → Hopper — key differences
NVLink/NVSwitch: topology, direct GPU-GPU access, peer mappings, bandwidth

topics.label: FS.3 Networking

subtopics.label: TCP/IP stack: Ethernet (frames), IP (routing, subnetting, CIDR), TCP (flow control, congestion control, Nagle, slow start, fast retransmit, SACK), UDP
BSD socket API: socket, bind, listen, accept, connect, send, recv, select/poll/epoll, non-blocking I/O
High-performance I/O: io_uring (completion queue, submission queue, link, splice, buffer selection), kernel bypass (DPDK, XDP)
InfiniBand: RC (reliable connection), UC, UD; RDMA verbs (send/recv, read/write, atomic); subnet manager
RoCE v2: RDMA over Converged Ethernet, PFC, DCQCN, ECN marking
NCCL: all-reduce (ring, tree/rabenseifner), all-gather, reduce-scatter, topology-aware communication

topics.label: FS.4 Compilers

subtopics.label: Compiler pipeline: lexer → parser → AST → semantic analysis → IR → optimization → code generation
LLVM: IR (SSA, basic blocks, phi nodes), optimization passes (mem2reg, GVN, inlining, loop unrolling, DCE), target description, MC layer, JIT (ORC JIT)
MLIR: dialects (linalg, affine, scf, func, arith, vector), operation semantics, pass pipeline, dialect conversion, pattern rewriting
XLA: HLO → optimization → LLVM IR, fused ops, layout assignment, Sharding for TPU/GPU
TVM: Relay IR, auto-tuning (AutoTVM, Ansor), schedule templates, code generation for CPU/GPU/MCU
Triton compiler: TIR → TritonGPU dialect → LLVM IR, block-level analysis, shared memory allocation, automatic pipeline

topics.label: FS.5 Data Science & ML fundamentals

subtopics.label: Scikit-learn ecosystem: pipelines, ColumnTransformer, cross-validation, GridSearchCV, RandomizedSearchCV, custom transformers, metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, F1, ROC-AUC)
Linear models: OLS (closed-form, gradient descent), ridge regression (L2), lasso (L1, coordinate descent), logistic regression (cross-entropy, multi-class one-vs-rest/softmax)
Tree-based methods: decision trees (CART, Gini/entropy, pruning), random forests (bagging, out-of-bag), gradient boosting (AdaBoost, XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost), feature importance
Support Vector Machines: primal/dual formulation, hinge loss, kernel trick (RBF, polynomial, sigmoid), SVR, soft-margin C parameter
Clustering: k-means (k-means++ initialization, elbow/silhouette), DBSCAN (epsilon, minPts), hierarchical/agglomerative (linkage criteria), Gaussian mixture models (EM algorithm)
Dimensionality reduction: PCA (SVD, explained variance, whitening), kernel PCA, t-SNE (perplexity, Barnes-Hut), UMAP, feature selection (filter/wrapper/embedded)

Time series analysis: stationarity (ADF test), ARIMA/SARIMA, exponential smoothing (Holt-Winters), seasonality decomposition (STL), Prophet, feature engineering (lags, rolling windows, Fourier features)

resources.label: Mathematics for Machine Learning (Deisenroth, Faisal, Ong) — free at mml-book.github.io
Linear Algebra Done Right (Axler) — conceptual depth, proof-based
3Blue1Brown Essence of Linear Algebra (YouTube) — intuition
MIT 18.06 Linear Algebra (Strang) — lectures on SVD, eigen
Modern Statistics: Intuition, Math, Python, R (Mike X Cohen) — code-first exercises
Probability and Statistics for Computer Science (Forsyth) — practical, code-heavy
Bayesian Data Analysis (Gelman et al.) — MCMC, model comparison
Convex Optimization (Boyd & Vandenberghe) — free online, chapters 1-5, 9
Numerical Optimization (Nocedal & Wright) — comprehensive reference
Adam optimization paper (Kingma & Ba, 2014)
Numerical Recipes (Press et al.) — reference for numerical methods
Cleve Moler's Numerical Computing with MATLAB (free online, concepts language-agnostic)
Operating Systems: Three Easy Pieces (Remzi & Andrea Arpaci-Dusseau) — free online
Linux Kernel Development (Love) — practical Linux internals
CS 162 (UC Berkeley) lectures on YouTube
Computer Architecture: A Quantitative Approach (Hennessy & Patterson) — chapters 1-3, 5
Computer Organization and Design (Patterson & Hennessy) — RISC-V edition
What Every Programmer Should Know About Memory (Drepper) — Linux memory deep dive
Programming Massively Parallel Processors (Kirk & Hwu) — GPU architecture chapters
NVIDIA CUDA Programming Guide — occupancy, memory hierarchy
Hopper H100 Architecture Whitepaper (NVIDIA)
TCP/IP Illustrated (Stevens) — chapters 1-3, 12-13, 17-18
Beej's Guide to Network Programming (Beej) — practical sockets in C
Lord of the io_uring (YouTube, ScyllaDB) — architecture and API
NCCL documentation (NVIDIA) — collective operations
Engineering a Compiler (Cooper & Torczon) — chapters 1-5, 8, 10
LLVM Kaleidoscope tutorial (llvm.org) — build a toy compiler front-to-back
MLIR: A Compiler Infrastructure for the End of Moore's Law (Lattner et al., 2019)
TVM documentation (tvm.apache.org) — getting started, auto-tuning
Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras, and TensorFlow (Géron) — practical from-scratch implementations
Introduction to Statistical Learning (James, Witten, Hastie, Tibshirani) — free at statlearning.com
Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning (Bishop) — PRML, the canonical ML theory text
Elements of Statistical Learning (Hastie, Tibshirani, Friedman) — free at hastie.su.domains/ElemStatLearn
Scikit-learn documentation (scikit-learn.org) — user guide, API reference, examples
Forecasting: Principles and Practice (Hyndman & Athanasopoulos) — free at otexts.com/fpp3

projects.label: Implement SVD from scratch in NumPy, use it to compress an image, blog the derivation
Implement QR decomposition via Gram-Schmidt, benchmark against NumPy
Implement MCMC (Metropolis-Hastings) from scratch to sample from a posterior; verify against analytical solution
Compute KL-divergence between two distributions and interpret
Implement Adam optimizer from scratch in NumPy, verify against PyTorch on a logistic regression task
Implement RK4 ODE solver; simulate double pendulum and compare energy conservation vs symplectic integrator
Write a C++ program that creates processes via fork(), communicates via shared memory, and measures context switch latency
Write a C++ program that deliberately causes TLB misses and cache misses; use perf stat to measure them; explain results
Profile a matrix multiplication kernel at different block/tile sizes using Nsight Compute; plot occupancy vs performance
Write a TCP echo server and client in C++ with epoll; benchmark throughput; extend with io_uring; compare results
Write a minimal expression evaluator in LLVM IR (compile + JIT); then write a Triton kernel for elementwise addition; compare against handwritten CUDA
Implement linear regression from scratch (OLS closed-form + gradient descent), compare coefficients against scikit-learn on a real dataset
Implement decision tree (CART) from scratch in Python, compare against scikit-learn on UCI datasets, visualize decision boundaries
Implement PCA from scratch via SVD, apply to MNIST for 2D visualization, compare with scikit-learn's PCA
Forecast a time series using ARIMA (auto-arima for order selection), compare with Prophet, evaluate on held-out period

topics.label: α.1 Transformer architectures, SSMs, MoE, multimodal models

subtopics.label: Attention: scaled dot-product, multi-head, cross-attention, causal masking, flash attention
Positional encodings: sinusoidal, learned, RoPE, ALiBi, relative position bias
Transformer variants: encoder-only (BERT), decoder-only (GPT), encoder-decoder (T5), prefix LM
State-space models: S4 (structured state space), Mamba (selective SSM), Mamba-2
Mixture-of-Experts: sparse MoE layers, top-k routing, load balancing loss, expert parallelism
Multimodal: vision encoders (ViT, SigLIP), cross-attention fusion, VLA models (RT-2, Octo, PaLM-E)
KV cache: caching, memory management, PagedAttention, continuous batching
Long context: sparse attention, sliding window, dilated attention, ring attention for >1M tokens

topics.label: α.2 Scaling laws, training dynamics, data curation, evaluation

subtopics.label: Scaling laws: loss vs param count, Chinchilla optimal compute, data scaling (Muennighoff), compute-optimal frontier
Training dynamics: warmup (linear/cosine), LR schedules, batch size scaling, gradient noise scale, μP (maximal update parameterization)
Data curation: deduplication (MinHash LSH, Bloom filters, particle dedup), quality filtering (fastText, perplexity), decontamination
Evaluation: perplexity, downstream tasks (MMLU, HumanEval, GSM8K, MATH, BigBench, HELM), LLM-as-judge, calibration (ECE)
Emergent abilities, grokking, double descent, phase changes in scaling
Synthetic data: self-instruct, evol-instruct, rejection sampling, model-generated data for training

topics.label: α.3 Distributed training (FSDP, DeepSpeed, Megatron)

subtopics.label: Data parallelism (DDP): all-reduce gradients, gradient accumulation, communication overhead
FSDP: sharding params/grads/optimizer states, hybrid sharding (HSDP), resharding strategies
DeepSpeed ZeRO stages (1/2/3): optimizer state partitioning, gradient partitioning, parameter partitioning, ZeRO-Offload, ZeRO-Infinity
Tensor parallelism: column/row linear splits, Megatron-LM strategy, communication volume analysis
Pipeline parallelism: GPipe, 1F1B schedule, interleaved scheduling, bubble size analysis
Mixed precision: fp16/bf16 training, gradient scaling, loss scaling, master weights
Activation checkpointing: selective, full, layer-wise, recomputation vs memory trade-off
Overlapping comm/compute: async all-reduce, double buffering, pipeline flush

topics.label: α.4 GPU programming (CUDA, Triton, CUTLASS)

subtopics.label: CUDA memory hierarchy: global, shared, registers, local memory; coalesced vs strided access patterns
Thread hierarchy: grid → block → warp → thread; occupancy, warp divergence, bank conflicts on shared memory
CUDA kernels: vector addition, matrix multiplication (naive → tiled → with shared memory → 1D/2D block tiling)
Triton: block-level programming model, pointer arithmetic, automatic tile scheduling, TMA (Hopper)
CUTLASS: template-based GEMM, hierarchical tiling, epilogue fusion (bias + ReLU), mixed precision
Kernel fusion: fusing elementwise ops, horizontal vs vertical fusion, flash attention as case study
Profiling: Nsight Compute (kernel-level: occupancy, memory, instruction stalls, roofline), Nsight Systems (timeline: streams, memcpy, kernel overlap)

topics.label: α.5 Numerical stability (mixed precision, gradient accumulation)

subtopics.label: fp16 vs bf16 vs fp32: dynamic range, precision, overflow/underflow behavior
Loss scaling: static vs dynamic, gradient histogram monitoring, overflow detection
Gradient accumulation: effective batch size, BN statistics updates, loss scaling interaction
Stochastic rounding, weight decay with mixed precision, master weights (fp32 copy)
Numerical debugging: loss spikes, NaN detection, gradient clipping (norm vs value), log softmax stability
Log-sum-exp trick, safe softmax, numerically stable cross-entropy

topics.label: α.6 Inference optimization (quantization, distillation, pruning, serving)

subtopics.label: PTQ: INT8/INT4/NF4 quantization, symmetric vs asymmetric, per-tensor vs per-channel vs per-group
QAT: fake quantization, straight-through estimator, learned step size (LSQ), quantization-aware fine-tuning
GPTQ: approximate Hessian-based weight quantization, group size, dampening
AWQ: activation-aware weight quantization, per-group scaling, equivalent transformation
Distillation: logit-based (KD), feature-based (FitNets), on-policy vs off-policy, sequence-level distillation
Pruning: unstructured (magnitude, SparseGPT), structured (attention head pruning, layer removal)
vLLM: PagedAttention, continuous batching, KV cache management, prefix caching
TensorRT-LLM: graph optimization, plugin kernels, in-flight batching, INT4/FP8 quantization

topics.label: α.7 Reinforcement learning (PPO, SAC, RLHF, DPO, sim-to-real, multi-agent RL)

subtopics.label: Classic RL: MDPs, policy gradient theorem, REINFORCE, advantage estimation (GAE)
PPO: clipped surrogate objective, adaptive KL penalty, trust region, PPO in continuous action spaces
SAC: maximum entropy RL, twin Q-networks, automatic temperature tuning, soft policy evaluation
RLHF: reward modeling, PPO in language (KL penalty, reward normalization, advantage whitening), InstructGPT pipeline
DPO: direct preference optimization, closed-form policy, no reward model needed, iterative DPO
Sim-to-real: domain randomization (visual, dynamics, mass/inertia), system identification, disturbance injection
Multi-agent RL: CTDE, MADDPG, QMIX, MAPPO, communication under bandwidth constraints

topics.label: α.8 Data pipelines (tokenization, streaming, filtering, curation)

subtopics.label: Tokenization: BPE (byte-pair encoding), WordPiece, Unigram, SentencePiece, tiktoken (OpenAI tokenizer), byte-level BPE
Streaming datasets: webdataset (tar-based shards, pipe mode), Mosaic StreamingDataset, datatrove, multi-worker
Quality filtering: fastText quality classifier, CCNet pipeline, perplexity filtering (with reference LM), heuristic filtering
Deduplication: MinHash LSH (document-level, Jaccard similarity), exact dedup (hash-based), Bloom filters, SimHash
Data mixing: proportions for pretraining vs domain-specific vs instruction tuning, curriculum learning
Data versioning: DVC, Hugging Face Datasets, Parquet/Arrow columnar format, data integrity checks

resources.label: Vaswani et al. 'Attention Is All You Need' — original transformer paper
Harvard NLP The Annotated Transformer — code walkthrough
Gu & Dao 'Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces'
Shazeer et al. 'Outrageously Large Neural Networks' — MoE origins
RoPE (Su et al.), ALiBi (Press et al.) papers
RT-2, PaLM-E, CLIP, LLaVA papers for multimodal fusion
Kaplan et al. 'Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models'
Hoffmann et al. 'Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models' (Chinchilla)
Scaling Data-Constrained Language Models (Muennighoff et al., 2023)
Deduplicating Training Data Makes Language Models Better (Lee et al., 2022)
MMLU, HumanEval, HELM evaluation frameworks — understand the benchmarks
PyTorch FSDP official tutorial + walkthrough
DeepSpeed documentation + ZeRO papers (Rajbhandari et al.)
Efficient Large-Scale Language Model Training on GPU Clusters Using Megatron-LM (Narayanan et al.)
Reducing Activation Recomputation in Large Transformer Models (Korthikanti et al.)
NVIDIA NCCL documentation — all-reduce, all-gather, reduce-scatter primitives
NVIDIA CUDA Programming Guide — chapters 1-5 mandatory
Programming Massively Parallel Processors (Kirk & Hwu) — best textbook
CUDA Mode YouTube lectures (Mark Saroufim) — practical walkthroughs
Triton official tutorials (triton-lang.org) — first 4 tutorials
CUTLASS documentation + examples — study the GEMM template
Mixed Precision Training (Micikevicius et al., 2017) — the original paper
NVIDIA AMP (Automatic Mixed Precision) documentation
Training Deep Networks with Loss Scaling — practical considerations
GPTQ: Accurate Post-Training Quantization for Generative Pre-trained Transformers (Frantar et al.)
AWQ: Activation-aware Weight Quantization for LLM Compression (Lin et al.)
SparseGPT: Massive Language Models Can Be Accurately Pruned in One-Shot (Frantar & Alistarh)
vLLM: Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention
TensorRT-LLM documentation + release notes
Proximal Policy Optimization Algorithms (Schulman et al., 2017)
Soft Actor-Critic (Haarnoja et al.)
Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback (Ouyang et al., InstructGPT)
Direct Preference Optimization (Rafailov et al.)
Spinning Up in Deep RL (OpenAI) — PPO, SAC implementations
TRL library (Transformer Reinforcement Learning) — hands-on RLHF
CCNet: Extracting High Quality Monolingual Datasets from Web Crawl Data (Wenzek et al., 2020)
The Pile: An 800GB Dataset of Diverse Text for Language Modeling (Gao et al., 2020)
webdataset GitHub + documentation — streaming for large-scale training
Hugging Face Datasets library — practical data loading
DataComp-LM (2024) — controlled data experiments for LLMs

projects.label: Implement a minimal GPT-2 decoder block (attn + FFN + RoPE) in PyTorch; train a tiny language model on Shakespeare to verify loss decreases
Implement a Mamba block (SSM + selective scan) and compare inference throughput against a transformer at the same parameter count
Train 3 small transformers (70M, 150M, 300M params) on a fixed dataset, plot loss vs compute, verify Chinchilla scaling prediction
Train a 1B param model on 2+ GPUs using FSDP; log throughput (tokens/sec), GPU utilization, memory; compare DDP vs FSDP vs ZeRO-3
Write a fused attention kernel in CUDA and Triton; benchmark against PyTorch's scaled_dot_product_attention across various sequence lengths
Train the same model in fp32 and bf16; log gradient histograms every 100 steps; identify and fix any numerical divergence
Quantize a LLaMA-7B to INT4 with GPTQ; deploy with vLLM; benchmark throughput and latency at batch sizes [1, 4, 16, 32]
Train a PPO agent in MuJoCo (HalfCheetah), record reward curve; then fine-tune a small LLM with DPO on a preference dataset; compare alignment approaches
Download 10GB CommonCrawl sample → quality filter → deduplicate → tokenize → stream to training loop; log throughput and loss curves

topics.label: β.1 Classical and modern control (PID, LQR, LQG, MPC, robust)

subtopics.label: PID: proportional/integral/derivative, tuning (Ziegler-Nichols, Cohen-Coon), integral windup, anti-windup
State-space: continuous vs discrete, controllability, observability, canonical forms (controllable, observable, Jordan)
LQR: cost function design (Q/R weights), solving algebraic Riccati equation, gain scheduling
LQG / Kalman + LQR: separation principle, loop transfer recovery (LTR)
MPC: receding horizon, QP formulation, constraint handling (input/state), real-time iteration (RTI)
Robust control: H∞, gain/phase margin, uncertainty modeling, mu-synthesis (concept level)
Implementation: discrete-time controllers, fixed-point arithmetic, sample rate selection

topics.label: β.2 State estimation (KF, EKF, UKF, particle filters, factor graphs)

subtopics.label: Bayes filter derivation: prediction + update, Markov assumption, Gaussian vs non-Gaussian filtering
Kalman Filter: process/measurement model, covariance propagation, innovation, Kalman gain computation
Extended KF: Jacobian linearization (first-order Taylor), EKF for non-linear dynamics, implementation
Unscented KF: sigma points, unscented transform, no Jacobian needed, UT parameters (α, β, κ)
Particle filter: importance sampling, resampling (multinomial, systematic, stratified, residual), SIR filter, degeneracy
Factor graphs: GTSAM, iSAM2, nonlinear least squares optimization, marginalization, smoothing
Multi-sensor fusion: loosely coupled vs tightly coupled, asynchronous sensor handling, outlier rejection (Mahalanobis distance)

topics.label: β.3 SLAM (visual SLAM, LiDAR SLAM, visual-inertial, multi-agent)

subtopics.label: Front-end: feature extraction (ORB, SIFT, SuperPoint, SuperGlue), feature matching, RANSAC, ICP (point-to-plane, GICP)
Back-end: pose graph optimization, iSAM2, g2o, GTSAM, loop closure detection (BoW, NetVLAD, ScanContext)
Visual SLAM: monocular (ORB-SLAM3), stereo, RGB-D; scale ambiguity, initialization, IMU preintegration
Visual-inertial: VINS-Mono, VINS-Fusion, OKVIS, MSCKF — IMU preintegration theory, marginalization
LiDAR SLAM: LOAM (scan matching, lidar odometry), A-LOAM, Cartographer (submap, scan-to-submap, loop closure)
Dense SLAM: KinectFusion (TSDF), octomap (probabilistic occupancy), ElasticFusion, RGB-D SLAM
Multi-agent SLAM: shared maps, relative pose estimation, communication constraints, CCM-SLAM

topics.label: β.4 Sensor modeling (IMU, GPS, cameras, LiDAR, radar, star trackers)

subtopics.label: IMU: MEMS vs tactical grade, accelerometer/gyro noise (Allan variance), bias instability, scale factor, misalignment
GPS/GNSS: pseudorange, carrier phase, RTK (real-time kinematic), DGPS, multipath effects, urban canyon
Cameras: pinhole model, intrinsic/extrinsic/distortion (radial/tangential), rolling shutter vs global shutter
LiDAR: time-of-flight, beam divergence, multi-echo return, 2D vs 3D (Velodyne, Ouster, Hesai solid-state)
Radar: Doppler velocity, FMCW, point cloud vs detection list, weather robustness, micro-Doppler
Star trackers: centroiding, star catalog matching (lost-in-space), attitude quaternion output, accuracy vs FOV
Sensor calibration: intrinsic (camera calibration with checkerboard), extrinsic (LiDAR-camera, IMU-camera), temporal sync

topics.label: β.5 Rigid-body dynamics (kinematics, Newton-Euler, Lagrangian, quaternions)

subtopics.label: 3D/2D transformations: rotation matrices, homogeneous transforms, SE(3), twist coordinates, se(3) algebra
Quaternions: multiplication, rotation, interpolation (SLERP), angular velocity ↔ quaternion derivative
Forward kinematics: DH parameters, Denavit-Hartenberg convention, product of exponentials (screw theory)
Inverse kinematics: analytical (6-DOF spherical wrist), numerical (Jacobian pseudoinverse, damped LS, Levenberg-Marquardt)
Velocity kinematics: geometric/analytical Jacobian, singularities, manipulability ellipsoid, null-space motion
Dynamics: Newton-Euler recursive algorithm (O(n)), Lagrangian formulation (mass matrix, Coriolis, gravity), Featherstone's ABA (O(n))
Contact and friction: Coulomb, viscous, Stribeck; contact forces, soft vs hard contact models

topics.label: β.6 Trajectory optimization and planning (RRT*, direct collocation, behavior trees)

subtopics.label: Sampling-based planning: RRT, RRT*, PRM, informed RRT*, bias towards goal, lazy collision checking
Optimization-based: direct collocation, direct transcription (trapezoidal, Hermite-Simpson), indirect (shooting method)
Smoothing: minimum-jerk, minimum-snap, cubic/quadratic B-splines, Dubins curves, Reeds-Shepp curves
Behavior trees: control flow (sequence, fallback, parallel), decorators (inverter, repeat, retry), blackboard, reactive behaviors
State machines: hierarchical state machines, states vs transitions, UML statechart, behavior-driven design
Collision checking: GJK (convex hulls), SAT (axis-aligned), BVH (bounding volume hierarchy), SDF (signed distance fields)
LQR-Trees: locally-linear feedback policies, stabilizable region, trajectory library

topics.label: β.7 Physics simulation (MuJoCo, Isaac Sim, Gazebo, sim-to-real)

subtopics.label: MuJoCo: model structure (MJCF), actuators, sensors (IMU, force, camera), contacts (elliptic, pyramidal friction cone), >=10 kHz physics
Gazebo: SDF/URDF models, plugins (sensor, system, visual), sensor simulation (GPU LiDAR, depth camera, IMU), ROS 2 integration
Isaac Sim: NVIDIA Omniverse, USD-based scenes, PhysX 5 physics, RTX rendering, synthetic data generation, domain randomization
Sim-to-real: domain randomization (visual, dynamics, mass/inertia, friction), system identification, disturbance injection
Simulation fidelity: contact models, friction cone accuracy, compliance, joint limits vs real hardware behavior
Validation: sim-to-real gap measurement, trajectory replay on real hardware, regression metrics, offline policy evaluation

resources.label: Coursera Modern Robotics: Mechanics, Planning, and Control (Lynch) — course 3 on control
CMU 16-299 Introduction to Feedback Control Systems — designed for CS majors
Modern Control Engineering (Ogata) — PID, state-space, LQR chapters

Probabilistic Robotics (Thrun, Burgard, Fox) — KF, EKF, particle filter chapters
GTSAM documentation + examples — factor graph approach
A Tutorial on Graph-Based SLAM (Grisetti et al.)
ORB-SLAM3 paper + GitHub — study the full pipeline architecture
LOAM: Lidar Odometry and Mapping in Real-time (Zhang & Singh)
Cartographer documentation + Google research paper — ROS integration
Visual-Inertial Monocular SLAM with Map Reuse (Campos et al., ORB-SLAM3)
Cyrill Stachniss SLAM lectures (YouTube) — lectures 7-12 on full SLAM systems
The Inertial Navigation System (Woodman) — excellent IMU tutorial from Cambridge
Multiple View Geometry in Computer Vision (Hartley & Zisserman) — camera model chapters
Kalibr (ETH) — multi-sensor calibration library documentation
Allan variance: IEEE Standard Specification Guide for Fiber Optic Gyros
Modern Robotics (Lynch & Park) — free online, chapters 2-4 (kinematics), 8 (dynamics)
A Mathematical Introduction to Robotic Manipulation (Murray, Li, Sastry) — screw theory
Quaternion intuition: 3Blue1Brown (YouTube) or Visualizing Quaternions (Hanson)
Drake documentation — rigid-body dynamics engine, contact solving
Rapidly-Exploring Random Trees (LaValle, 1998)
Planning Algorithms (LaValle, 2006) — sampling-based planning chapter
Behavior Trees in Robotics and AI (Colledanchise & Ögren)
OMPL (Open Motion Planning Library) documentation — practical implementations
Minimum Snap Trajectory Generation for Quadrotors (Mellinger & Kumar)
MuJoCo documentation (mujoco.readthedocs.io) — getting started, modeling guide
MuJoCo Python bindings (dm_control) — examples
Gazebo + ROS 2 tutorials (gazebosim.org)
NVIDIA Isaac Sim documentation + sample projects
Domain Randomization for Transferring Deep Neural Networks (Tobin et al.)

projects.label: Implement PID, LQR, and MPC for a simulated inverted pendulum; compare rise time, overshoot, settling time, disturbance rejection
Implement 2D EKF from scratch for a robot with IMU + GPS + wheel odometry; plot covariance ellipses vs ground truth trajectory
Run ORB-SLAM3 on a KITTI sequence; plot trajectory against ground truth with evo; investigate and explain a failure case
Simulate IMU + GPS + camera sensors in Python with realistic noise models (Allan variance); run EKF fusing all three; analyze error growth during GPS outage
Implement forward/inverse kinematics for a 6-DOF arm in Python using MuJoCo; make the arm reach a target position in 3D space
Implement RRT* in Python for a 2D environment with obstacles; plan smooth minimum-snap trajectory through waypoints; simulate flight in PyBullet
Model a quadcopter in MuJoCo (mass, inertia, propellers); implement a PID stabilizing controller; transfer to Gazebo with sensory noise

topics.label: γ.1 Real-time C/C++ (deterministic memory, MISRA, interrupts, watchdog)

subtopics.label: Deterministic memory: no dynamic allocation after init, stack-only designs, memory pools, ring buffers, static allocation
MISRA C++ guidelines: rule categories (required, advisory), auto, templates, exceptions, RTTI restrictions
Interrupt handling: ISR design (short, reentrant, no blocking), nested interrupts, interrupt priorities, latency analysis
Watchdog timers: IWDG (independent) vs WWDG (windowed), petting patterns, deadlock detection
Real-time scheduling: fixed priority preemptive, rate monotonic, earliest deadline first, priority inversion and inheritance
Timing analysis: WCET (worst-case execution time), jitter, response time analysis, interference
Lock-free programming: atomic operations (compare-and-swap, fetch-add), memory ordering (acquire/release/seq-cst), lock-free queues
Static analysis: SonarQube, PVS-Studio, Clang Static Analyzer, Coverity for MISRA compliance

topics.label: γ.2 RTOS (FreeRTOS, Zephyr, QNX, VxWorks, RTEMS)

subtopics.label: FreeRTOS: tasks, queues, semaphores (binary/counting/mutex), timers, event groups, task notifications, config files
Zephyr: device tree (devicetree), bindings, kernel objects, build system (west and CMake), networking stack (Zephyr TCP/IP)
QNX: message passing, resource managers, adaptive partitioning, safety certification (IEC 61508, DO-178C)
VxWorks: tasks, semaphores, watchdog, priority inheritance, POSIX compliance, memory protection (MMU-based)
RTEMS (NASA missions): classic API vs POSIX API, multiprocessing, ED&D (executive, debug, development) support
RTOS design patterns: publisher-subscriber, producer-consumer, commander-executor, watchdog-supervisor, heartbeat-monitor
Hard vs soft real-time: deadline miss consequences, statistical guarantees, scheduling analysis

topics.label: γ.3 Embedded Linux (Yocto, Buildroot, device trees, kernel modules)

subtopics.label: Buildroot: minimal embedded Linux from source, menuconfig, rootfs overlay, cross-compilation toolchain, defconfig
Yocto: layers (meta-*, poky), bitbake recipes/classes/tasks, image/package/sdk generation, build performance
Device trees: .dts/.dtsi syntax, nodes, properties, binding documentation, overlays, dtc compiler
Linux kernel modules: hello world module, init/exit, module parameters, character device drivers, IOCTL, file_operations
Kernel configuration: menuconfig, kernel config fragments, building for target (ARM64, RISC-V, x86), defconfig
Boot process: U-Boot (SPL, FIT images, fastboot), kernel boot (decompress → setup → start_kernel), initramfs, systemd/busybox init
NVIDIA Jetson platform: JetPack SDK (L4T kernel, CUDA, TensorRT, cuDNN), power management (NV Power model), thermal throttling

topics.label: γ.4 Microcontrollers (ARM Cortex-M, RISC-V)

subtopics.label: ARM Cortex-M: NVIC (nested vector interrupt controller), SysTick, MPU (memory protection unit), bit-banding, sleep modes (sleep/stop/standby), CMSIS-Core
RISC-V: CLIC/PLIC, CLINT, S-mode vs M-mode privilege levels, vector extensions (V扩展), RISC-V debug spec
Memory-mapped I/O: registers base addresses, bit manipulation macros, volatile qualifier usage

Peripherals: GPIO, UART (sync/async, FIFO, flow control), I2C (multi-master, clock stretching), SPI (CPOL/CPHA modes), CAN, DMA, ADC, PWM, timer/counter (input capture, output compare, PWM generation)
Low-power design: sleep/wake cycles, clock gating (RCC), power domains (PD), battery life estimation, dynamic voltage/frequency scaling (DVFS)
Debugging: SWD/JTAG, semihosting, ITM (instrumentation trace macrocell) trace, Segger RTT, logic analyzer, oscilloscope
HAL vs bare-metal: STM32 HAL/LL (low-layer) drivers, Zephyr driver model, or register-level for deep understanding

topics.label: γ.5 FPGAs and hardware acceleration (HDL, HLS, AXI4)

subtopics.label: Digital logic: gates, flip-flops (D, JK, T), registers, counters, FSMs (Moore, Mealy), clock domains, metastability, synchronizers
Verilog/SystemVerilog: modules, always blocks (combinational vs sequential), blocking vs non-blocking assignments, testbenches, waveform simulation
FPGA architecture: LUTs (look-up tables), flip-flops, BRAM (block RAM), DSP48 slices (multiply-accumulate), PLLs (clock generation), I/O blocks (LVDS, single-ended)
HLS (Vivado HLS, Vitis HLS): C/C++ → RTL, pipelining (II=1), unrolling (factor), array partitioning, dataflow, interfaces (AXI4-Lite, AXI4-Stream, AXI4-MM)
CPU/FPGA integration: AXI4-Lite (control/status registers), AXI4-Stream (high-bandwidth data), DMA transfers, interrupts
Tool flow: simulation (ModelSim/Questa), synthesis, implementation (place and route), timing analysis (setup/hold), bitstream generation

topics.label: γ.6 Hardware-in-the-loop (HITL) testing

subtopics.label: HITL architecture: simulation environment + hardware device (target) + real-time communication bridge
Sim ↔ hardware: serial (RS-232, RS-422/485), CAN, EtherCAT, UDP, shared memory, PXIe backplane
SIL (software-in-the-loop): flight/controller software runs against model simulation on development host
PIL (processor-in-the-loop): compiled binary runs on target CPU within simulation context via debug interface
Real-time synchronization: hardware clock, simulation time stepping, time scaling factor, external clock reference
Failure injection: sensor dropout/disconnection, communication latency/jitter, actuator saturation, bit flips, brownout
Test automation: Jenkins/GitLab CI for HITL suites, regression test criteria (pass/fail metrics), automated report generation

topics.label: γ.7 Communication buses (CAN, SPI, I2C, UART, SpaceWire, EtherCAT, MIL-STD-1553)

subtopics.label: UART: async serial, start/stop bits, baud rate, parity (none/even/odd), RS-232 (<15m) vs RS-422/485 (<1200m, differential)
I2C: multi-master, open-drain, clock stretching, 7-bit vs 10-bit addressing, bus capacitance (max devices), standard/fast/high-speed modes
SPI: full-duplex, master/slave, chip select, clock polarity/phase (CPOL/CPHA), daisy-chaining, quad-SPI (QSPI)
CAN: 2.0A (11-bit ID) vs 2.0B (29-bit ID), CSMA/CA + arbitration, bit stuffing, ACK slot, error states (active/passive/bus-off), CAN-FD (flexible data rate)
EtherCAT: master/slave (processing on the fly), datagram processing on the wire, distributed clocks (DC), ECO mode, hot connect
SpaceWire: LVDS (low-voltage differential signaling), wormhole routing, packet switching, time-codes, data-strobe encoding, RMAP protocol
MIL-STD-1553: command/response protocol, bus controller + up to 31 remote terminals, 1 MHz Manchester encoding, transformer-coupled

topics.label: γ.8 Edge inference (TensorRT, ONNX Runtime, TFLite Micro, TVM)

subtopics.label: TensorRT: network definition from ONNX, layer fusion, FP16/INT8 calibration (entropy, percentile), dynamic shapes, DLA (deep learning accelerator)
ONNX Runtime: ONNX model format, execution providers (CPU, CUDA, TensorRT, OpenVINO, CoreML), model optimization (level 1-3), custom ops
TFLite Micro: interpreter runtime, operator resolver (all ops vs selective), arena-based memory, delegates for hardware acceleration
TVM: Relay IR → schedule → auto-tuning (AutoTVM, Ansor, MetaSchedule) → code generation, microTVM for MCU, target-specific schedules
Custom operator deployment: writing TensorRT plugins (CUDA C++, IPluginV2), ONNX Runtime custom ops (kernel + schema), TFLite Micro custom ops
Memory constraints: model size vs hardware RAM, quantization + memory mapping, off-chip memory (flash), execution in place
Benchmarking: latency (preprocessing → inference → postprocessing), throughput (QPS), power consumption (Joules/inference), thermal throttling effect

resources.label: Real-Time C++: Efficient Object-Oriented and Template Microcontroller Programming (Kormanyos)
MISRA C++:2023 guidelines (reference with AI, not cover-to-cover)
FreeRTOS documentation: Implementing a Watchdog Timer section
A Practitioner's Approach to Real-Time Computing (Buttazzo) — scheduling theory
FreeRTOS official tutorials + reference manual — start here
Mastering FreeRTOS using STM32 (Udemy) — hands-on with hardware
Zephyr documentation — build the example applications
Hands-On RTOS with Microcontrollers (Amos, 2nd ed) — cross-RTOS patterns
RTEMS Classic API Guide (NASA) — for space domain awareness
Buildroot documentation + examples — start with Raspberry Pi or QEMU target
Yocto Project Development Manual — chapters 1-5
Mastering Embedded Linux Programming (Simmonds, 3rd ed)
Linux Kernel Module Programming Guide (online, free)
NVIDIA Jetson Developer Guide — building custom kernel, flashing
STM32 Nucleo + CubeIDE tutorials — practical start
The Definitive Guide to ARM Cortex-M3 and Cortex-M4 (Yiu) — architecture reference
ARM Cortex-M4 Technical Reference Manual — register map
STM32 reference manual (specific to your MCU)
The RISC-V Reader (Patterson & Waterman) — concise architecture intro
FPGA Programming for Beginners (Packt) — Verilog + Vivado hands-on
Digital Design and Computer Architecture (Harris & Harris) — chapters 1-4 (digital logic, Verilog)
Vivado Design Suite User Guide — synthesis, implementation, timing
Vitis HLS documentation + tutorials — C++ → RTL examples
ZipCPU blog (zipcpu.com) — excellent practical FPGA/CPU debugging
NASA cFS HITL examples — open source, space-hardened patterns
dSPACE documentation — commercial HIL market leader, learn the architectural concepts
Pixhawk HITL setup — PX4 SITL/HITL documentation
Hardware-in-the-Loop Testing (Defense Technical Information Center) — survey paper
Understanding each bus — start with Wikipedia overview + logic analyzer captures
CAN Specification 2.0 (Bosch) — arbitration and error handling chapters
SPI / I2C: Logic Analyzer in Action tutorials (Saleae blog)
EtherCAT: The Complete Guide (Beckhoff) — free
SpaceWire: ECSS-E-ST-50-12C standard (ESA)
TensorRT Developer Guide — ONNX import, INT8 calibration, dynamic shapes
ONNX Runtime tutorials — Python + C++ inference examples
TFLite Micro documentation — porting to MCU, operator support
TVM documentation + microTVM (tvm.apache.org)
Efficient Processing of Deep Neural Networks (Sze et al.) — DNN accelerator design book

projects.label: Write a FreeRTOS task in C++ that reads IMU via I2C, runs an EKF, and logs to SD — with watchdog, no dynamic allocation, verified with static analysis
Port the IMU+EKF+SD logger to Zephyr; compare API differences between FreeRTOS and Zephyr
Build a custom embedded Linux image with Buildroot for Raspberry Pi; write a kernel module that reads GPIO button presses via /dev
Blink an LED, then read IMU via I2C using only register-level access (no HAL); implement a software I2C bit-bang to verify protocol understanding
Implement a hardware FIR filter in Verilog; simulate with testbench; synthesize for a dev board; confirm filter output matches Python reference
Set up SITL → compile for Raspberry Pi → connect via UDP to Gazebo simulation; inject a GPS dropout and observe estimator drift; verify failover
Connect an IMU to STM32 via I2C and SPI (separate sensors); read and log both; use a logic analyzer to decode the bus traffic and verify protocol
Export ResNet-18 to ONNX; deploy with TensorRT on Jetson INT8; deploy same model on MCU with TFLite Micro; compare latency and accuracy

topics.label: δ.1 Classical mechanics (Lagrangian, Hamiltonian, perturbation theory)

subtopics.label: Lagrangian mechanics: generalized coordinates, Euler-Lagrange equations, conservation laws, Noether's theorem
Hamiltonian mechanics: Legendre transform, Hamilton's equations, phase space (q,p), Poisson brackets, Liouville's theorem
Rigid-body dynamics: Euler angles, angular momentum, inertia tensor, Euler's equations, gyroscopic precession
Orbital perturbation theory: J2 (oblateness), atmospheric drag, solar radiation pressure, third-body perturbations, Lagrange planetary equations
Celestial mechanics: Kepler problem, elliptical orbits, mean/true/eccentric anomaly, Lambert's problem, orbit determination (Gibbs, Lambert)
Chaos and resonance: separatrix crossing, resonance overlap (Chirikov criterion), Lyapunov exponents, KAM theorem (concept)

topics.label: δ.2 Electromagnetism (Maxwell, radiation, antenna theory)

subtopics.label: Maxwell's equations: differential and integral forms, divergence/curl, charge conservation, boundary conditions
EM waves: wave equation in vacuum/matter, polarization (linear/circular/elliptical), Poynting vector, wave impedance
Radiation: dipole radiation (Hertzian dipole), antenna gain, directivity, effective area, Friis transmission equation
Propagation: free-space path loss, atmospheric attenuation (rain, oxygen, water vapor), multipath (ground bounce), Fresnel zone
Antenna types: dipole, patch (microstrip), helical, phased array, parabolic dish, Yagi-Uda — basic characteristics and radiation patterns
Spacecraft EM: plasma interactions, spacecraft charging (surface/internal), EMI/EMC, Faraday cage, grounding/bonding

topics.label: δ.3 Plasma physics (MHD, particle-in-cell methods)

subtopics.label: Plasma fundamentals: Debye length, plasma frequency, gyroradius (Larmor radius), collision frequency, quasi-neutrality
MHD equations: continuity, momentum (with pressure and Lorentz force), energy, induction equation, frozen-in flux theorem
MHD waves: Alfvén waves, magnetosonic (fast/slow) waves, group/phase velocity diagrams
PIC: particle pushing (Boris algorithm), field solve on Yee grid, current deposition, Monte Carlo collisions
Space plasmas: solar wind (fast/slow), magnetosphere (bow shock, magnetopause, tail), ionosphere (D/E/F layers), magnetic reconnection
Sheaths and double layers: Debye sheath formation, Bohm criterion, presheath, Child-Langmuir law

topics.label: δ.4 Numerical PDEs (FDM, FVM, spectral methods)

subtopics.label: Finite difference: explicit vs implicit, CFL condition, von Neumann stability analysis, FTCS, Crank-Nicolson, ADI (alternating direction implicit)
Finite volume: conservation laws (integral form), flux reconstruction, Riemann solvers (Godunov, HLLC, Roe), slope limiters (minmod, MC, Superbee)
Spectral methods: Fourier series (periodic domains), Chebyshev polynomials (non-periodic), Galerkin vs collocation, dealiasing (3/2 rule, 2/3 rule)
Mesh generation: structured vs unstructured, adaptive mesh refinement (AMR), octrees, quadtrees, Delaunay triangulation
Time integration: RK4, symplectic integrators (Stormer-Verlet, leapfrog), IMEX (implicit-explicit), Rosenbrock methods
Boundary conditions: Dirichlet, Neumann, Robin, periodic, absorbing (PML — perfectly matched layer)

topics.label: δ.5 PINNs and operator learning (DeepONet, FNO)

subtopics.label: PINNs: loss function (PDE residual + BC + data), collocation points, automatic differentiation, gradient balancing
Training strategies: LR schedules, gradient balancing (NTK, soft-attention, adaptive weights), curriculum learning (residual-based refinement)
DeepONet: branch net (input function encoder) + trunk net (query coordinates), operator learning, stacked/unstacked architectures
FNO: spectral convolution in Fourier space, lifting/projection layers, resolution invariance, grid-free after training
Applications: inverse problems, surrogate modeling for PDE-constrained optimization, design optimization
Limitations: spectral bias (Fourier feature encoding), failure on stiff PDEs, high-frequency modes, training instability

topics.label: δ.6 Scientific computing (HPC, MPI, GPU PDE solvers)

subtopics.label: HPC patterns: domain decomposition, MPI (message passing — point-to-point, collectives, Cartesian topology), OpenMP (shared memory, tasks, SIMD), hybrid MPI+OpenMP
GPU acceleration: CUDA PDE solvers, grid/block decomposition, shared memory tiling, halo exchange (ghost cells), kernel fusion
Parallel I/O: HDF5 (hierarchical data format, chunking, compression, collective I/O), NetCDF, ADIOS2 (BP format, SST streaming)
Stencil computation: Jacobi, Gauss-Seidel, red-black Gauss-Seidel, multigrid (V-cycle, W-cycle), Roofline model (arithmetic intensity, bandwidth bound)
Performance profiling: MPI profiling (PAPI, Score-P, Vampir), GPU profiling (Nsight, NVProf), scaling (Amdahl's law, Gustafson's law)
Containerization: Docker (for development), Singularity/Apptainer (HPC, shared filesystem, GPU support), Charliecloud

topics.label: δ.7 Astrophysics data analysis (time-series, image reconstruction, Bayes)

subtopics.label: Time-series: Lomb-Scargle periodogram (unevenly spaced data), Box-fitting Least Squares (transits), wavelets (continuous, discrete), autoregressive models
Image reconstruction: deconvolution (Richardson-Lucy), CLEAN algorithm (radio synthesis), MEM (maximum entropy), PSF modeling, aperture synthesis
Bayesian inference: MCMC (emcee, PyMC3, Pyro), nested sampling (MultiNest, dynesty, UltraNest), Gaussian processes (GP regression for time-series)
Spatial statistics: point process models, kriging (Gaussian process regression for spatial data), dust map inference, clustering (DBSCAN, HDBSCAN)
Multi-messenger astronomy: gravitational wave + EM counterpart identification, time-domain alerts (VOEvent, LVAlert), sky localization
Tools: AstroPy (FITS, coordinates, convolution, cosmology), SunPy (solar data analysis), AstroML (datasets and examples), CASA (radio interferometry)

resources.label: Classical Mechanics (Goldstein) — chapters 1-3 (Lagrangian), 8-9 (Hamiltonian) — use AI tutor
Orbital Mechanics for Engineering Students (Curtis) — two-body problem, perturbations
Methods of Celestial Mechanics (Brouwer & Clemence) — perturbation theory reference
Introduction to Electrodynamics (Griffiths) — chapters 7-9 (Maxwell's equations, EM waves)
Antenna Theory and Design (Balanis) — chapters 1-4 (fundamentals, dipoles, arrays)
Spacecraft Charging (Garrett & Whittlesey) — AIAA progress series
Introduction to Plasma Physics (Chen) — chapters 1-5 (fundamentals, waves)
Plasma Physics for Astrophysics (Priest) — space applications
PIC Methods in Plasma Physics (Birdsall & Langdon) — computational approach
Boris algorithm — derivations and implementations (many blog posts)
Numerical Recipes (Press et al.) — PDEs, ODEs, FFT chapters
Computational Fluid Dynamics (Ferziger & Perić) — FVM approach
Finite Difference Methods for ODEs and PDEs (LeVeque)
Spectral Methods in Fluid Dynamics (Canuto et al.) — chapters 1-3
Physics-informed neural networks (Raissi et al., 2019) — original PINNs paper
DeepONet: Learning Nonlinear Operators via DeepONet (Lu et al.)
Fourier Neural Operator for Parametric PDEs (Li et al.)
Scientific Machine Learning online course (SciML, 2023) — lecture series
Introduction to High Performance Computing for Scientists and Engineers (Hager & Wellein)
MPI tutorial (mpitutorial.com) — collective ops, Cartesian topology
CUDA C++ Best Practices Guide — grid/block heuristics, memory optimization
HDF5 documentation — data model, Python and C APIs
Roofline model: Williams et al. — insightful visual performance model
Statistics, Data Mining, and Machine Learning in Astronomy (Ivezić et al.) — comprehensive
Lomb-Scargle Periodograms (VanderPlas, 2018) — AJ article, free online
Bayesian Data Analysis (Gelman et al.) — MCMC, model comparison chapters
AstroML documentation (astroml.github.io) — examples and datasets
SunPy documentation (sunpy.org) — solar physics data analysis

projects.label: Simulate a satellite orbit with J2 perturbation in Python; plot nodal precession over 100 orbits
Build a Python link budget calculator for a CubeSat communication downlink (frequency, distance, antenna gains, modulation)
Implement a 1D electrostatic PIC code: initialize a two-stream instability, observe the growth of the unstable mode
Solve 2D heat equation using finite difference (explicit and implicit); compare stability and runtime; implement adaptive time stepping
Solve Burgers' equation with a PINN in PyTorch; compare against finite-difference reference; try Fourier feature encoding for high-frequency resolution
Write an MPI-parallel 2D heat equation solver in C++; run on 1, 2, 4, 8 processes; plot strong/weak scaling efficiency
Download solar wind data from NASA CDAWeb; compute Lomb-Scargle periodogram; build a Gaussian process model to predict solar wind speed with uncertainty intervals

topics.label: ε.1 Cloud / AWS (EC2, S3, VPC, Lambda, IAM)

subtopics.label: EC2: instance types (GPU: p4d, p5; CPU: c6i, m6i), AMIs, security groups, elastic IPs, spot instances (interruption handling)
S3: buckets, objects, storage classes (Standard, IA, Glacier), lifecycle policies, presigned URLs, S3a/ExpressOne, replication (CRR/SRR)
VPC: subnets, route tables, NAT gateway/gateway, security groups vs NACLs (stateless vs stateful), VPC peering, endpoints (gateway/interface)
IAM: users, groups, roles, policies (AWS-managed vs customer-managed vs inline), trust relationships, least privilege, instance profiles
Lambda: serverless functions, triggers (S3, SQS, API Gateway, DynamoDB Streams), cold starts (VPC vs no VPC), reserved concurrency, layers
GPU-specific: EFA (Elastic Fabric Adapter, GPUDirect RDMA), placement groups (cluster/partition/spread), FSx for Lustre, EBS gp3/io2 performance

topics.label: ε.2 Infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, Kubernetes, Docker)

subtopics.label: Docker: Dockerfile best practices (multi-stage builds, layer caching, .dockerignore), Compose (services, networks, volumes), buildx for multi-arch
Kubernetes: pods, deployments, StatefulSets, services (ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer), ConfigMaps, Secrets, volumes (emptyDir, hostPath, PVC), Ingress, Helm charts
K8s for ML: GPU scheduling (NVIDIA device plugin, time-slicing, MIG), Volcano (batch scheduling, queue, podgroup), Kubeflow (pipelines, notebooks, Katib, KFServing)
Terraform: HCL syntax, modules, state management (local vs remote backends — S3+DynamoDB), providers, resource lifecycle (create, read, update, delete), workspaces
GitOps: ArgoCD (declarative Git-sync, sync waves, prune), Flux (Kustomize, Helm, notification controller), continuous sync, drift detection
Cluster lifecycle: Kops (AWS), kubeadm, K3s (edge/lightweight), EKS/AKS/GKE (managed), Bottlerocket OS, Cluster API (declarative cluster management)
Container security: image scanning (Trivy, Clair), runtime security (Falco, seccomp, AppArmor), pod security standards (privileged, baseline, restricted), network policies

topics.label: ε.3 CI/CD (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, pytest, GTest)

subtopics.label: GitHub Actions: workflow syntax (jobs, steps, matrix strategy), self-hosted runners (actions-runner-controller), caching (actions/cache, dependencies), artifacts
GitLab CI: .gitlab-ci.yml (stages, jobs, rules, artifacts, dependencies), runners (shared, specific, group), DAG scheduling (needs), CI/CD for K8s
Python testing: pytest (fixtures scopes, parametrize, conftest, markers), coverage (pytest-cov, diff-cover), tox (multi-version), hypothesis (property-based)
C++ testing: GTest (test fixtures, assertions (EXPECT/ASSERT), death tests, typed tests), CMake integration (enable_testing, GTest discovery), CTest/CDash
CI for ML: data validation (TFX), model evaluation gate (compare baseline vs candidate on eval set), W&B integration (diff metrics), approval step
Docker-in-CI: building images with Kaniko/buildkit (no Docker daemon), pushing to ECR/DockerHub, integration tests in ephemeral containers
Secrets management: GitHub Secrets (encrypted env vars), GitLab CI variables (masked, file, group), HashiCorp Vault (dynamic secrets, kv engine), Doppler

topics.label: ε.4 Build systems (CMake, Bazel, PlatformIO)

subtopics.label: CMake: targets (executable, library, custom), properties (target-specific, directory), find_package (config vs module mode), FetchContent (external dependencies), toolchain files (cross-compilation), install rules (export, DESTINATION)
CMake + modern C++: INTERFACE/SHARED/STATIC targets, alias targets (namespace), generator expressions ($<…>), target_include/properties/link_* commands
Bazel: BUILD files (rules: cc_binary, cc_library, cc_test, py_binary), dependencies (deps, data, runtime_deps), remote caching (buildbarn, nginx + S3), remote execution (buildfarm)
PlatformIO: embedded build system for MCU, board definitions (boards/), library management (platformio.ini, lib_deps), unit testing on target, CI integration
Cross-compilation: CMake toolchain files (CMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE, set compiler/sysroot/arch), Bazel --config (platform mapping, toolchain resolution), PlatformIO board profiles
Package managers: Conan (C++ — conanfile.txt, conanfile.py, remote repos), vcpkg (C++ — manifests, triplets), pip/conda (Python), npm/yarn (Node.js), conan + CMake integration

topics.label: ε.5 Data pipelines (Kafka, Ray, distributed data loading)

subtopics.label: Kafka: topics, partitions (keyed vs round-robin), producers (acks, idempotent, compression), consumer groups (assign vs subscribe, rebalance), offsets (auto/manual), replication (ISR, min.insync.replicas), schema registry (Avro, Protobuf, JSON Schema)
Kafka Streams: KStream/KTable/GlobalKTable, state stores (RocksDB), exactly-once semantics, processor API, interactive queries

Ray: Core (tasks, actors, object store, placement groups), Ray Data (distributed dataset, map/batch/inference/config), Ray Train (distributed training orchestration), Ray Serve (model composition, autoscaling, batching)

Distributed data loading: webdataset (tar-based shards, caching, shard shuffle), Mosaic StreamingDataset (online shuffling, mix of datasets), NVIDIA DALI (GPU-accelerated data loading and augmentation pipeline)
Performance: data access bottlenecks (CPU decode vs GPU idle), prefetching (DoubleBuffer, prefetch_factor), shared memory, NUMA-aware data loading, HDD vs SSD vs NVMe bandwidth

topics.label: ε.6 Open-source collaboration (Git, PR etiquette, licenses, documentation)

subtopics.label: Git deep: rebase vs merge, interactive rebase (squash, fixup, reword), bisect (binary search for regressions), blame, reflog (recovery), submodules vs subtrees
PR etiquette: atomic commits (single concern), descriptive titles, linking issues (Fixes #…), changelogs, responding to review (resolve, reply, update), small focused PRs

Open-source licenses: MIT (permissive), Apache 2.0 (permissive + patent grant), GPLv3 (copyleft), LGPL (library copyleft), AGPL (network copyleft), BSD (2/3-clause), license compatibility, choosing the right one

Documentation: README (structure: what, why, how, quickstart), API docs (Sphinx for Python, Doxygen for C++, JSDoc for TypeScript), developer guide (CONTRIBUTING.md), Sphinx-Napoleon for Google/NumPy docstrings

Release management: semantic versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH), release notes (Keep a Changelog, github-changelog-generator), tags (git tag -s signed), CI/CD for releases, artifact publishing (npm, PyPI, Cargo, Docker Hub)
Community: code of conduct (Contributor Covenant), issue triage (labels, milestones, stale bot), reviewing others' PRs (be kind, constructive), becoming a maintainer, CHANGELOG-driven development

topics.label: ε.7 Experiment tracking (Weights & Biases, MLflow)

subtopics.label: W&B: runs, config (hyperparameters), metrics (loss, accuracy, gradient histograms), artifacts (datasets, models), sweeps (grid, random, Bayesian, hyperband), reports (tables, charts, markdown), alerts

MLflow: tracking server (runs, params, metrics, tags, artifacts), model registry (versions, stage transitions, lineage, signatures), MLflow Projects (conda/Docker environments), MLflow Recipes (step-by-step pipelines)
Comparison: W&B vs MLflow vs Comet vs Neptune vs DVC vs TensorBoard — when each fits (team size, deployment model, experiment volume, artifact storage)

Reproducibility: full environment capture (pip freeze > requirements.txt, conda env export, Docker + commit hash), seed management (random, numpy, torch.cuda), deterministic algorithms (torch.use_deterministic_algorithms)
Metrics: loss/accuracy curves, gradient histograms (distribution over time), parameter distribution (weights, biases), GPU utilization, throughput (samples/sec), memory bandwidth, PCIe utilization
Visualization: parallel coordinates (hyperparameter effects), contour plots (2D parameter landscape), learning rate finder, confusion matrix, ROC (receiver operating characteristic) curves, PR curves

resources.label: AWS Well-Architected Framework — six pillars (operational excellence, security, reliability, performance efficiency, cost optimization, sustainability)
AWS documentation: EC2 GPU instances, EFA setup for multi-node training
AWS in Action (Wittig & Wittig) — practical hands-on
IAM policy simulator — test policies before deploying
Docker documentation — getting started, Dockerfile best practices
Kubernetes in Action (Luksa, 2nd ed) — chapters 1-10
Terraform: learn.hashicorp.com tutorials — AWS provider, modules
Kubeflow documentation — end-to-end ML on K8s
NVIDIA GPU Operator documentation — deploying GPU nodes with MIG
GitHub Actions documentation + starter workflows

Build a pipeline: streaming sensor data from Kafka → preprocess with Ray Data → write to Parquet; benchmark throughput at different batch sizes
Contribute a meaningful PR to an open-source robotics or ML library (ROS2, ORB-SLAM3, PyTorch, HuggingFace, etc.) that passes review and merges
